title: Soulful Japa: Session 51 : Be Jubilant: Supreme Lord is a Person!
speaker: HG Madhu Pandit Dasa

segments.text: Jai Shila Prabhupada! Hare Krishna Nama Prabhu Ki Jai!
And welcome all of you for the 51st session of Soulful Japa.
So yesterday we had missed out to do our one round of chanting with Prabhupada.
So we'll start with that only in the morning.
So let's do our first round of concurrent chanting come hearing.
Chanting and hearing exactly the same word being directed by Shila Prabhupada,
commanded by Shila Prabhupada.
As we go on chanting, Prabhupada intercepts us and says,
he gives the golden commands to chant and hear exactly the same word.
When you chant, you should hear. While you chant, you should hear.
You chant and hear the Hare Krishna. Then your mind gets locked up.
All those four quotes are coming along the way.
So those who are new, I'm just explaining. Please follow that.
As soon as Prabhupada's commands come, you just be obedient and follow that.
That is a way to train ourselves with this.
Mindful Japa. Please start.
Exactly the same sound you hear.
You simply hear. When you say Hare Krishna, you try to hear the very sound.
You simply chant Hare Krishna and try to hear the sound. That's all.

Consciousness is not a independent reality.

What is final truth?

That is all.
That is all.
There is no Shakti which you cannot describe.
Because the moment you describe, then that is part of this material world.
So this is called Nirguna Brahman.
So from Shankaracharya, Buddhism which says everything is Shunya, Shankaracharya brought
one step up.
No, no, no, no.
There is some Brahman.
There is some spirit.
There is some consciousness which is different from matter.
So he postulated that consciousness.
Shankaracharya is the one who first postulated consciousness.
In Buddhism, there is no consciousness.
Consciousness is a result of just material energy.
Whereas in what Shankaracharya said, he postulated, he took out from the Vedas and proved from

Independent of material nature.
That is what he said.
That is what he said.
That is what he said.
That is what he said.
That is what he said.
That is what he said.
That is what he said.
That is what he said.

They are called Mayavadi.

That's all.

I am eternal servant of Krishna.

It is eternal.
So you cannot become God, but you become that elemental energy and material people

So anybody if you talk about an identity, if you talk about a name, if you talk about

It is not Param Satya.
It is Maya.
Param Satya means no form, no identity, no feelings.
no longer a person, you merge into impersonalism. So, that is the conclusion of Shankaracharya's

That is a Vedic aphorism. Aham Brahmasmi, I am Brahman. What it means according to Vaishnavas
is, yes, I am of the quality of Brahman. I am Brahman. And I also know there is Parabrahman.
There is a Supreme Brahman. Now they say Aham Brahmasmi, I am that Brahman, I am that Supreme.
Right?
Aham Brahmasmi, Tatto Masi. Tatto Masi, I am that, means I am that quality. Just like
if you say, I am Indian and the Prime Minister is Indian, so I am Prime Minister. So, this
kind of interpretations have been given.
Same verse, same thing is taken and God is impersonal.
So but please understand Padma Purana has got evidence where under Lord Vishnu's order,
he is coming and doing it in order to re-establish Vedas into the society after Buddhism had
taken over all over the world.
So there was a purpose and that purpose is served after that Lord Vishnu is all Krishna
is in control of this existence.
We had Ramanujacharya, Madhvacharya, they are all Acharyas who brought in.
Took the same Vedanta Sutra and said, proved, no, no, no, what Shankaracharya said is wrong.
Param Satya absolute truth what you can deduce from this scripture is that God is a person.
There is a supreme person and we are all his amshas, part and parcel.
So personalist philosophers, philosophy came back, came into India, became prevalent.
Shri Sampradaya, Ramanujacharya.
became very prominent, Madhvacharya became very prominent.
Then Chaitanya Mahaprabhu extended this.
He took, you know, initiation in the parampara of Madhvacharya and then he extended that

Achintya, Veda, Veda, Abheda, Tattva, Veda and Abheda.
We are one with and different from God.
We are one with God because we are his energy.
Effect has cause in it always.
Cause always has effect in it.
So in that way, we are caused by God, therefore, you know, we are not totally separated from
God.
We are part and parcel of God.
So in this way, our quality, we are qualitatively one with God.
But quantity wise, God is unlimited, I am limited.
So this is the philosophy of Chaitanya Mahaprabhu.
This is the philosophy of Vaishnavism, all Vaishnava Sampradayas have same philosophy.
God is the supreme person.
There is a Parabrahman and there is Brahman.
Unlimited Brahmans are there.
There is one Parabrahman.
And that Parabrahman is Krishna.
That Parabrahman is Narayana, Vishnu, that category, Vishnu Tattva.
All the Sampradayas agree that there is a category called Vishnu Tattva.

That excited me, theoretical physics.

Objective world.

Philosophy of, you know, what's the absolute truth and all these things.

I went through it.

What is, look at the number of stars on the sky, whether I live or die, what makes it,

There's no meaning at all to this existence.

That.
